ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general tofu skin food product class. No specific entry exists for truffle-infused tofu skin rolls; closest_parent match used. Truffle oil and mushroom filling components are not individually coded.

ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00GJ covers soybean curd products broadly. No specific code exists for yuba rolls with truffle oil; closest_parent match used.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of Truffle-Infused Tofu Skin Rolls is approximately 120 g, equivalent to 3–4 individual rolls as typically plated in a high-end Chinese restaurant or dim sum setting.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Tofu, raw (FDC ID 172443) and Mushrooms, white, raw (FDC ID 169251), combined with soy-based product reference data; truffle oil contribution estimated from olive oil base (FDC ID 171413)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exists for truffle-infused tofu skin rolls. Nutrient values are estimated by component: tofu skin (yuba) nutritional profile anchored to USDA FDC tofu and soy product data; mushroom filling anchored to USDA white mushroom data; truffle oil contribution estimated from olive oil base. Input AI-generated values (confidence 0.9) were cross-checked against these USDA component references and found to be broadly consistent. Values retained with source match type 'estimated'. Saturated fat estimated at approximately 1.5 g/100 g based on soy and mushroom fat composition. Cholesterol is 0 mg as all ingredients are plant-based.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Soy and Legume-Based Foods — analogous estimate based on tofu GI data; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for truffle-infused tofu skin rolls. GI estimated at 25 (low) based on tofu and soy protein food analogues from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). Tofu skin (yuba) is high in protein and fat with very low carbohydrate content (~5.8 g/100 g), consistent with a low GI response. Mushroom filling contributes negligible glycemic load. Glycemic load per 120 g serving = (25 × 5.8 × 1.2) / 100 = 1.74, rounded to 1.7.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content at 310 mg/100 g (372 mg per 120 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should be mindful that a 120 g serving provides approximately 372 mg sodium, primarily from soy sauce in the filling; requesting reduced-sodium preparation or limiting portion size is advisable to keep daily sodium within the recommended 2000 mg ceiling.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~25) and low carbohydrate content (5.8 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Truffle-Infused Tofu Skin Rolls have a low estimated GI of approximately 25 and a glycemic load of only 1.7 per 120 g serving, making them a suitable protein-rich, low-glycemic option for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when prepared without added sweeteners.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Plant-based protein, low saturated fat (~1.5 g/100 g), and soy isoflavone content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The dish provides plant-based soy protein with a favorable unsaturated-to-saturated fat ratio and soy isoflavones that may support endothelial function; however, the sodium content warrants attention for individuals with concurrent hypertension and cardiovascular disease.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Soy isoflavones and mushroom antioxidants with potential anti-inflammatory properties
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Soy protein isoflavones and the antioxidant compounds in mushrooms may offer modest anti-inflammatory benefits; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis can include this dish as part of an anti-inflammatory dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Truffle-Infused Tofu Skin Rolls (黑松露腐皮卷, Hēi Sōng Lù Fǔ Pí Juǎn) are a sophisticated contemporary Chinese appetizer that elevates the traditional dim sum staple of yuba (fresh tofu skin) with premium black truffle oil. The dish is constructed by wrapping thin, delicate sheets of tofu skin around a savory filling of finely minced button mushrooms, sometimes blended with wood ear or other fungi for added textural contrast. The rolls are then lightly steamed or pan-seared to achieve a tender yet slightly firm bite, and finished with a drizzle of high-quality black truffle oil that imparts an intense, earthy aroma. The flavor profile combines the subtle nuttiness of yuba, the deep umami of mushrooms, and the luxurious, forest-floor fragrance of truffle oil. Nutritionally, per 100 g the dish provides approximately 145 kcal, 9.5 g protein, 10.2 g fat (predominantly unsaturated), and only 5.8 g carbohydrate, making it a high-protein, low-carbohydrate, plant-based option. The estimated glycemic index is low (~25), and a 120 g serving carries a glycemic load of only 1.7, making it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium content is moderate at 310 mg/100 g, primarily from soy sauce seasoning, which is a consideration for hypertension management. The dish is fully vegan and vegetarian, soy-allergen-bearing, and free of cholesterol. It is popular in modern, high-end Chinese restaurants and contemporary dim sum menus seeking to blend traditional Chinese textures with premium international ingredients such as black truffle.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for truffle-infused tofu skin rolls as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component references (tofu/soy products, white mushrooms, olive oil base for truffle oil) and cross-checked against the AI-generated input values (confidence 0.9). GI is an analogous estimate anchored to tofu and soy food data from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008. Confidence is therefore rated 'medium' per Project Veritas v2 policy.
notes: 1. NUTRIENT SOURCE: No single USDA FoodData Central or HPB Singapore entry exists for this composite dish. Values estimated from USDA component references: tofu skin anchored to USDA FDC tofu/soy data (FDC ID 172443), mushroom filling to USDA white mushroom data (FDC ID 169251), truffle oil contribution estimated from olive oil base (FDC ID 171413). Input AI-generated values were broadly consistent with these references and retained. 2. SATURATED FAT: Estimated at 1.5 g/100 g based on soy and mushroom fat composition from USDA FoodData Central; not reported in input. 3. MONOUNSATURATED / POLYUNSATURATED FAT: Estimated at 4.8 g and 3.2 g per 100 g respectively, based on soy and olive oil fatty acid profiles from USDA FoodData Central. 4. TRANS FAT: 0.0 g — confirmed negligible for plant-based soy and mushroom preparation with olive-oil-based truffle oil. 5. CHOLESTEROL: 0.0 mg — confirmed zero as all ingredients are plant-based (soy, mushrooms, plant oil); naturally zero for this vegan preparation. 6. VITAMIN A: 0.0 mcg RAE — naturally zero; tofu skin and mushrooms contain no preformed vitamin A and negligible beta-carotene. 7. VITAMIN B12: 0.0 mcg — naturally zero; this is a fully plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients. 8. WATER: Set to -1 (data not available); moisture content for this specific composite preparation is not reported in USDA FoodData Central or any other approved source consulted as of 2026-04-17. 9. GI: Analogous estimate of 25 (low) based on tofu and soy protein food analogues from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). No direct GI measurement for this dish exists in approved sources. 10. ONTOLOGY: FoodOn closest_parent match to tofu skin food product class; FoodEx2 closest_parent match to A00GJ (soybean curd products). No exact codes exist for this fusion preparation. SNOMED CT: no applicable code identified. 11. MULTILINGUAL: This is a Chinese Contemporary dish, not a Singaporean dish; Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names are not applicable. Chinese-script name confirmed: 黑松露腐皮卷. 12. PORTIONS: Input contained a duplicate portion entry (two identical 120 g entries); deduplicated to a single default serving of 120 g. 13. SODIUM: At 310 mg/100 g (372 mg per 120 g serving), sodium is moderate and primarily attributable to soy sauce seasoning in the filling; relevant for hypertension management.
